zh.lifeSummary: 當Robert de Marmion, Lord of Scrivesby and Tamworth於1040年出生在法國諾門迪卡爾瓦多斯豐特納勒-馬米時，他的父親William Marmion35歲，母親Lesceline le Goz15歲。他和Hawise至少有4個兒子和1個女兒。他去世於1106年在他的家鄉享年66歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Marmion,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish: perhaps an Anglicized form of an unidentified Gaelic name. Compare   Merriman  .
English (Lancashire; of Norman origin) and French: nickname from Old French marmion ‘monkey, brat’.

variants.name: Merriman
meanings: English:

nickname from Middle English mirie, merie, murie ‘merry, pleasant’ + man ‘man’. Compare Merry 1. This surname is established in Ireland (perhaps as an Anglicized form of an unidentified Gaelic name; compare Marmion ) as well as England.

there has probably been some confusion with Marriman, probably an occupational name meaning ‘servant of Mary’, from the female personal name Mary (see Marie ) + Middle English man in the sense ‘servant’, or perhaps a pet form of Mary using the hypocoristic suffix -man. The surname Marriman is now vanishingly rare in Britain and Ireland.

variants.name: Marmon
meanings: English (Lancashire and Gloucestershire):
variant of   Marmion  .
variant of Marriman, an occupational name meaning ‘servant of Mary’, from the female personal name Mary (see Marie ) + Middle English man in the sense ‘servant’.

nameMeanings.name: Lord
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English laford, louerd, lord ‘lord, master’ (Old English hlāford), perhaps given to a someone who played the part of the lord or master of ceremonies in festival games.
Irish: adoption of the English name (see 1 above) as a translation of the main element of Gaelic Ó Tighearnaigh (see Tierney ) and Mac Thighearnáin (see McKiernan ).
Americanized form of French Laure, a variant of the personal name Laur 3.

variants.name: Tierney
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Eamon, John Patrick, Brigid, Clodagh, Kieran, Michael Sean, Siobhan.

Irish: shortened form of O'Tierney, an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Tíghearnaigh ‘descendant of Tighearnach’, a byname meaning ‘lord, master’. The Gaelic name is sometimes ‘translated’ as English Lord . Compare Tiernan .

variants.name: Lords
meanings: English: variant of Lord , with post-medieval excrescent -s. This surname is rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Korean Kyong, Kyong Suk, Young, Chae, Chi Young, Hang, Kil, Rin, Sang Hun, Chang, Chong, Insoo, Kum, Sun Young, Yom, Young Hwan.  Cambodian Kiwan, Oeurn, Phoeun, Pich, Saroeuth, Seung, Sophath, Sophea, Yoeun, Phan, Son.  Indian Shanti, Harendra.
Indian (Uttar Pradesh): Brahmin name, from Sanskrit om, the Hindu sacred and mystic syllable uttered at the beginning of prayers.
Cambodian: written ឱម, corresponding to the exclamation of solemn affirmation, of Sanskrit origin (see 1 above), chanted by Buddhist monks.

variants.name: Oum
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Southeast Asian Heang, Hye, Kam, Kan, Kheng, Khin, Kun, Sang, Sok, Phong, Yom, Yoo, Youn, Cuong, Phal, Samnang, Tin.  Cambodian Sokha, Chheang, Choeun, Moeun, Roeun, Sambath, Samoeun, Savuth, Soeun, Sopheap.
Cambodian: written អ៊ុំ, from a Khmer word denoting an elder sibling of one's parent (i.e. ‘uncle’ or ‘aunt’). Compare   Om   3 and   Um   2.

nameMeanings.name: and
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of French Canadian Hénault or directly of French Hunault (see Henault 2).
Catalan (Añó): Castilianized form of Anyó, probably from the old personal name Annone.
Japanese: variant of   Anno  .

variants.name: Anno
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Japanese Masayuki, Tadashi, Yoshito.
Japanese: written 安野 ‘peaceful plain’. This surname is rare in Japan proper, and may have originated in the Ryūkyū Islands, where it is pronounced   Ano  .

variants.name: Henault
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Andre, Emile, Lucien, Martial, Pierre, Camille, Fernand, Laurier, Monique, Normand, Philippe.
French (Hénault): from the ancient Germanic personal name Haginwald, composed of the elements hagin ‘enclosure, protected place’ and wald ‘power, authority’.
Altered form (Hénault) of French Hunault: from the ancient Germanic personal name Hunwald, composed of the elements hun ‘bear cub’ and wald ‘power, authority’. Compare Ano and Eno .

nameMeanings.name: Tamworth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: locative name from Tamworth (Staffs).

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

en.lifeSummary: When Robert de Marmion, Lord of Scrivesby and Tamworth was born in 1040, in Fontenay-le-Marmion, Calvados, Normandy, France, his father, William Marmion, was 35 and his mother, Lesceline le Goz, was 15. He had at least 4 sons and 1 daughter with Hawise. He died in 1106, in his hometown, at the age of 66.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Robert de Marmion, Lord of Scrivesby and Tamworth est né en 1040, à Fontenay-le-Marmion, Calvados, Normandie, France, son père, William Marmion, avait 35 ans et sa mère, Lesceline le Goz, avait 15 ans. Il a eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille avec Hawise. Il est décédé en 1106, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 66 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Robert de Marmion, Lord of Scrivesby and Tamworth 1040 in Fontenay-le-Marmion, Calvados, Normandie, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater William Marmion 35 und seine Mutter Lesceline le Goz 15. Er hatte mit Hawise mindestens 4 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb 1106 im Alter von 66 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Robert de Marmion, Lord of Scrivesby and Tamworth è nato il nel 1040 nel Fontenay-le-Marmion, Calvados, Normandie, Francia, il suo padre, William Marmion, aveva 35 anni e il suo madre, Lesceline le Goz, aveva 15 anni. Lui avuto almeno 4 figli e 1 figlia con Hawise. Morì nel 1106, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 66 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Robert de Marmion, Lord of Scrivesby and Tamworthが1040年にフランスノーマンディカルヴァドスフォンテナイ-レ-マルミオンで生まれた時，父William Marmionは35歳，母Lesceline le Gozは15歳であった。彼はHawiseとの間に少なくとも4人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1106年に故郷で66歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Robert de Marmion, Lord of Scrivesby and Tamworth(이)가 1040년 프랑스 노먼디 캘바도스 폰텐아이-레-마미언에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William Marmion(은)는 35세였고, 그의 어머니 Lesceline le Goz(은)는 15세였다. 그는 Hawise(와)과 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1106년 그의 고향에서 66세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Robert de Marmion, Lord of Scrivesby and Tamworth nasceu em 1040, em Fontenay-le-Marmion, Calvados, Normandie, França, seu pai, William Marmion, tinha 35 anos e sua mãe, Lesceline le Goz, tinha 15 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 4 filhos e 1 filha com Hawise. Ele faleceu em 1106, em sua cidade natal, com 66 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert de Marmion, Lord of Scrivesby and Tamworth родился в 1040 г. в местности фонтенеи-ле-мармион, калвадос, норманди, Франция, его отцу по имени William Marmion было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали Lesceline le Goz, 15. У него было по крайней мере 4 сына и 1 дочь от Hawise. Он умер в 1106 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 66 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Robert de Marmion, Lord of Scrivesby and Tamworth nació en 1040, en Fontenay-le-Marmion, Calvados, Normandie, Francia, su papá, William Marmion, tenía 35 años y su mamá, Lesceline le Goz, tenía 15 años. Tuvo por lo menos 4 hijos y 1 hija con Hawise. Murió en 1106, en su pueblo, a la edad de 66 años.
